source_name: Code4rena Reserve Protocol Invitational Findings and Analysis Report (2023-07)
finding_title: RTokenAsset price oracle can return a huge but valid high price when any underlying collateral’s price oracle timeout
summary: The RTokenAsset is an implementation of interface IRTokenOracle to work as a oracle price feed for the little RToken. RTokenAsset implements the latestPrice function to get the oracle price and saved time from the cachedOracleData, which is updated by _updateCachedPrice function:
root_cause: RTokenAsset can return an extreme but valid high price when any underlying collateral oracle is timed out
broken_invariants: RTokenAsset pricing must propagate stale underlying oracle failures instead of producing valid-looking extreme prices
basket-level price aggregation must not mask one collateral timeout as a high valid RToken price
stale oracle handling must be consistent across nested collateral structures
attack_preconditions: the affected Reserve collateral/plugin/wrapper is deployed or configured in an RToken basket
the report-described oracle, reward, wrapper, or integration edge case is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, trader, or protocol participant able to trigger the affected pricing/trading/reward/withdrawal flow
trigger_actions: execute or wait for the report-described state transition
observe mispricing, reward misallocation, fund lock, or availability failure

impact.description: The RTokenAsset is an implementation of interface IRTokenOracle to work as a oracle price feed for the little RToken. RTokenAsset implements the latestPrice function to get the oracle price and saved time from the cachedOracleData, which is updated by _updateCachedPrice function:

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; impact, preconditions, or scope are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: verify the exact collateral plugin/wrapper is in scope and configured
check whether oracle freshness, decimals, or external integration assumptions differ in the current target
confirm the issue affects value movement, redemption availability, or reward ownership rather than a harmless view mismatch
mitigations: Add a BU price valid check in the RToken.tryPrice: function tryPrice() external view virtual returns (uint192 low, uint192 high) { (uint192 lowBUPrice, uint192 highBUPrice) = basketHandler.price(); // {UoA/BU} require(lowBUPrice != 0 && highBUPrice != FIX_MAX, "invalid price"); ### Assessed type Context
